products.name: Surgicover
products.description: A specialised peri-operative enteral supplement formulated to optimise surgical recovery, preserve lean muscle mass, and accelerate wound healing across all phases of surgery. It serves as a metabolic bridge throughout the surgical timeline — from pre-operative prehabilitation through home recovery.
products.target_audience: Hospitals, surgical centres, clinical dietitians, surgeons, B2B procurement teams, and post-surgical patients

variants.name: Vanilla
variants.description: Smooth, creamy flavour ideal for general patient compliance, especially for patients experiencing post-operative taste alterations

variants.name: Chocolate
variants.description: Rich flavour for patients with reduced appetite or post-operative taste changes (dysgeusia)

variants.name: Diabetic Cover
variants.description: Zero added sucrose, sweetened with sucralose — specifically safe for Type 1 and Type 2 diabetic and pre-diabetic surgical patients requiring strict glycaemic control

variants.name: Dry Fruits
variants.description: Mild, natural-tasting savory-sweet profile designed for patients experiencing post-operative anorexia, nausea, or taste alterations caused by medications, anaesthesia, and systemic inflammation

products.preparation: Measure 150ml of lukewarm water or milk into a clean container. Add exactly one heaped scoop (20g) of Surgicover powder. Stir or shake briskly until completely dissolved. Consume immediately after preparation. Recommended 1-2 servings daily or as directed by the attending clinical specialist.
products.preparation_warnings: Do NOT mix with hot or boiling liquids — temperatures above 40C / 104F denature soya and milk proteins (causing clumping) and degrade heat-sensitive vitamins (Vitamin C, B1, B2, B6, B9, B5). Use only lukewarm or room-temperature liquids. Do NOT consume within 2 hours before anaesthesia (nil-by-mouth NPO window) due to protein and fat content affecting gastric emptying.
products.ingredients: Soya Protein Isolate, Defatted Soya Flour, Skimmed Milk Powder, Sucralose (sweetener/INS955), Di-basic Calcium Phosphate, Cyanocobalamin (B12), Thiamine Mononitrate (B1), Riboflavin (B2), Niacinamide (B3), Calcium D-Pantothenate (B5), Pyridoxine Hydrochloride (B6), Cholecalciferol (Vitamin D), Folic Acid (B9), Ascorbic Acid (Vitamin C), Ferric Ammonium Citrate (Iron), Manganese Sulphate, Copper Sulphate, L-Arginine, L-Leucine, Preservative INS211, Permitted Flavours (Dry Fruits / Vanilla / Chocolate).
products.regulatory_label: CONTAINS NATURALLY OCCURRING SUGARS. Contains Milk and Soy allergens. Not for parenteral or intravenous use. Best stored in a cool, dry place away from direct sunlight. Claim: Zero Added Sugar (Sucrose) — lawful under FSSAI Non-Addition of Sugars guidelines because no monosaccharides or disaccharides have been added during manufacture; naturally occurring lactose from Skimmed Milk Powder is present. The brand cannot claim Diabetic-Friendly or Low GI without a formal human-subject GI Testing Report from an accredited lab under FSSAI 2026 audit standards.

nutrition_per_20g_serving.protein: 6.0g — PDCAAS 1.0 hybrid soya and milk protein (30g per 100g)

clinical_use.pre_op_prehabilitation_7_to_5_days: Start 5-7 days before elective surgery: 1 serving (20g in 150ml lukewarm water or milk) twice daily — mid-morning and before bed. Goal: build protein reserves, optimise micronutrient levels, strengthen immune system ahead of surgical stress. Delivers 400mg L-Arginine and 200mg L-Leucine per day at BID dosing. Especially beneficial for patients with BMI under 18.5, >10% unintentional weight loss in 6 months, or scheduled for major gastrointestinal, orthopaedic, or oncological procedures.
clinical_use.pre_op_window_24_to_2_hrs: 24-6hrs before surgery: light, low-residue dinner permitted; solid foods stopped 6hrs pre-surgery. 6-2hrs before surgery: clear carbohydrate-rich beverage under explicit clinical supervision only (to stimulate insulin, preserve glycogen, reduce post-op insulin resistance). Within 2hrs of surgery: strict nil-by-mouth (NPO) — Surgicover MUST NOT be consumed in this window due to protein and fat content.
clinical_use.post_op_day_1_clear_liquids: Post-Op Day 1 (Phase A): Early oral feeding with clear liquids only — water, coconut water, clear strained vegetable broth. Surgicover not yet introduced at this stage.
clinical_use.post_op_days_2_to_5_soft_diet: Post-Op Days 2-5 (Phase B): As patient transitions to soft or pureed diet, introduce Surgicover at 1 serving (20g in 150ml lukewarm water) twice daily — mid-morning and evening. Prevents gastric distension in post-anaesthetic gastroparesis while delivering continuous amino acid support. Compatible patient foods: plain yogurt, mashed bananas, khichdi, pureed lentils.
clinical_use.post_op_days_6_to_14_and_beyond: Post-Op Days 6-14+ (Phase C): Patient transitions to standard high-protein diet. Continue 1-2 servings of Surgicover daily. Powder can be dissolved in lukewarm milk, water, or blended into pureed oatmeal or warm porridges to enhance compliance during extended home recovery.
clinical_use.home_recovery_14_to_30_days: Maintain 1-2 servings daily for 14-30 days post-discharge for ongoing tissue remodelling, collagen maturation, and physical recovery. In patients undergoing physical therapy, administering Surgicover 20-30 minutes post-exercise optimises mTORC1-mediated muscle protein synthesis.

key_clinical_points: Zero added sucrose — prevents post-operative hyperglycaemia which impairs neutrophil chemotaxis, increases surgical site infection risk, inhibits collagen synthesis, and prolongs hospital stay
L-Arginine (200mg per serving, 1000mg per 100g) is the sole substrate for nitric oxide synthase — enhances wound perfusion, collagen deposition, fibroblast proliferation, and T-cell immune response
L-Leucine (100mg per serving, 500mg per 100g) activates mTORC1 pathway — directly stimulates myofibrillar protein synthesis and inhibits ubiquitin-proteasome muscle degradation
PDCAAS 1.0 protein — clinically equivalent to whey in amino acid quality with superior GI tolerance for post-operative patients (lactose-free primary protein source)
Lactose load less than 0.1g per 20g serving — far below the 12g clinical threshold for secondary lactose intolerance
Fractionated TID or QID dosing (3-4 x 20g per day) delivers 18-24g bioavailable protein per day without triggering muscle-full saturation or GI distress
Zero fat effectively (0.19g per serving) prevents delayed gastric emptying in early post-operative period
Aligned with ERAS (Enhanced Recovery After Surgery) protocols — validated across all major surgical departments
Soya protein globulins (beta-conglycinin and glycinin) exert anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, preserve gut mucosal barrier, and reduce systemic inflammation
Vitamin C (32mg) co-delivered with L-Arginine — essential cofactor for prolyl and lysyl hydroxylase in collagen triple-helix formation
Copper (0.32mg) required for lysyl oxidase — cross-links collagen and elastin to build wound tensile strength
Manganese (0.50mg) activates glycosyltransferases — essential for extracellular matrix synthesis during tissue remodelling

knowledge_sections.title: Why Soya Protein Instead of Whey
knowledge_sections.content: Post-operative patients experience transient gut barrier dysfunction and down-regulated brush border disaccharidases, leading to temporary secondary lactose intolerance especially in first 48-72 hours. Whey protein exposes these patients to residual lactose, causing bloating, osmotic diarrhoea, and abdominal distension that delay enteral feeding and worsen nitrogen balance. Soya Protein Isolate (SPI) is naturally lactose-free. SPI achieves PDCAAS 1.0 — equivalent to milk, egg, and casein. SPI contains beta-conglycinin and glycinin globulins with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects within the intestinal lumen, supporting gut-associated lymphoid tissue (GALT) and limiting inflammatory signal translocation. SPI also naturally contains high L-Arginine, complementing the free-form L-Arginine fortification. Skimmed Milk Powder is included as a minor component to provide methionine (lower in soy) and improve dispersibility and sensory characteristics. The lactose from SMP per 20g serving is less than 0.1g — well below the 12g clinical tolerance threshold for secondary lactose intolerance. Soya Protein Isolate is also approximately five times more cost-effective to produce than whey protein, directly improving patient compliance over the 14-30 day recovery period.

knowledge_sections.title: L-Arginine Mechanism — Nitric Oxide and Collagen Pathways
knowledge_sections.content: L-Arginine is the sole substrate for nitric oxide synthase (NOS): L-Arginine + O2 + NADPH → L-Citrulline + Nitric Oxide + NADP+. Plasma L-Arginine falls 30-50% below pre-operative levels within 48 hours of major surgery due to accelerated arginase II catabolism and elevated NOS demand at wound sites. Nitric oxide at the wound site mediates: (1) local vasodilation expanding the microvascular network to deliver oxygen, inflammatory cells, and nutrients to the hypoxic wound environment; (2) upregulation of VEGF stimulating angiogenesis and new capillary formation; (3) modulation of macrophage and T-lymphocyte activity. Beyond the NOS pathway, L-Arginine feeds the arginase pathway: L-Arginine → L-Ornithine → (a) polyamines via ornithine decarboxylase for fibroblast proliferation and tissue growth; and (b) L-Proline via ornithine aminotransferase — the primary structural amino acid for the collagen triple-helix. Collagen synthesis requires prolyl and lysyl hydroxylase co-catalysed by Vitamin C. Surgicover co-delivers L-Arginine (200mg per serving) with Vitamin C (32mg per serving) to provide complete collagen pathway support. Clinical trials show moderate L-Arginine daily doses (4.5-9g total) significantly reduce wound size, restore depressed hydroxyproline levels (index of collagen synthesis), increase wound breaking strength by over 40%, and increase T-cell immune responses. High doses over 14g per day offer no additional benefit. Surgicover delivers synergistic dosing combining free-form L-Arginine with the naturally high L-Arginine content of SPI to restore depleted plasma levels without GI discomfort.

knowledge_sections.title: L-Leucine Mechanism — mTORC1 and Muscle Preservation
knowledge_sections.content: Post-operative immobilisation, systemic inflammation, and inadequate protein intake create anabolic resistance — skeletal muscle fails to respond to normal amino acid elevations. Standard protein intake cannot stimulate muscle protein synthesis. L-Leucine is a branched-chain amino acid (BCAA) that bypasses hepatic first-pass metabolism, entering systemic circulation directly and being taken up by skeletal muscle. It acts as an intracellular nutrient sensor activating mTORC1 via the Sestrin2-GATOR2 complex, recruiting mTORC1 to the lysosomal membrane. Active mTORC1 phosphorylates: (1) p70S6K — promotes ribosomal translation initiation; (2) 4E-BP1 — relieves inhibition on translation initiation factor complexes. This directly stimulates myofibrillar protein synthesis independently of systemic insulin. L-Leucine also reduces muscle proteolysis by inhibiting ubiquitin-proteasome-mediated degradation. However, L-Leucine alone without a complete essential amino acid (EAA) pool cannot sustain protein synthesis — the body breaks down endogenous muscle tissue to acquire missing substrates. Surgicover pairs free L-Leucine (100mg per serving) with PDCAAS 1.0 Soya Protein Isolate to provide both the mTORC1 molecular trigger AND the complete EAA substrate pool. Soya protein contains approximately half the L-Leucine of whey — free-form fortification bridges this gap to reach the leucine threshold required for mTORC1 activation. Clinical evidence: leucine-enriched supplements preserve appendicular lean mass, improve handgrip strength, walking speed, and respiratory muscle function, reducing post-operative pulmonary complications.

knowledge_sections.title: Zero Added Sucrose and Glycaemic Control
knowledge_sections.content: Surgery triggers cortisol, glucagon, and catecholamine hypersecretion alongside pro-inflammatory cytokines (TNF-alpha, IL-1, IL-6), creating peripheral insulin resistance even in non-diabetic patients. Post-operative blood glucose can reach 10-14 mmol/L in otherwise healthy individuals. Consequences of hyperglycaemia: impaired neutrophil chemotaxis and phagocytic function (increased surgical site infection rate), inhibited collagen synthesis (reduced prolyl hydroxylase activity, impaired fibroblast proliferation), increased anastomotic leak risk, prolonged hospital stay. Sucrose (GI=65) added to a supplement in this state compounds existing hyperglycaemia. Surgicover uses sucralose exclusively as sweetener (GI=0, 0 kcal/g). Sucralose is not metabolised by glucose pathways — passes through the digestive tract unchanged, contributes zero calories and zero glycaemic load, does not raise blood glucose or stimulate insulin secretion. Surgicover is safe for Type 1 and Type 2 diabetic patients. The Diabetic Cover variant is specifically optimised for diabetic and pre-diabetic patients. Under FSSAI Non-Addition of Sugars guidelines, Zero Added Sugar (Sucrose) is lawful because no monosaccharides or disaccharides were added. The mandatory label disclosure is: CONTAINS NATURALLY OCCURRING SUGARS (referring to lactose from Skimmed Milk Powder). Note: Diabetic-Friendly and Low GI claims cannot appear on pack under FSSAI 2026 audit standards without accredited human-subject GI testing.

knowledge_sections.title: Micronutrient Roles in Surgical Recovery
knowledge_sections.content: Vitamin C (32mg per serving, 40% RDA): Essential cofactor for prolyl and lysyl hydroxylase in collagen triple-helix formation; co-delivered with L-Arginine for complete wound healing pathway support. Iron (2.4mg per serving, 7% RDA): Facilitates oxygen transport to wound site and is an obligate cofactor for collagen synthesis. Copper (0.32mg per serving, 19% RDA): Required for lysyl oxidase which cross-links collagen and elastin fibres to build wound tensile strength and prevent dehiscence. Manganese (0.50mg per serving, 12% RDA): Activates glycosyltransferases involved in extracellular matrix synthesis during tissue remodelling. Niacinamide B3 (8mg per serving, 44% RDA): Precursor to NAD+/NADH, driving cellular respiration required for energetic tissue remodelling. Pantothenic Acid B5 (0.67mg per serving, 13% RDA): Necessary for Coenzyme A synthesis, aiding lipid metabolism and cell division at wound site. Thiamine B1 (0.60mg per serving, 37% RDA): Essential cofactor for pyruvate dehydrogenase, enabling aerobic glucose metabolism in recovering tissues. Riboflavin B2 (0.73mg per serving, 37% RDA): Supports flavoprotein enzyme systems for electron transport and tissue repair. Pyridoxine B6 (0.32mg per serving, 13% RDA): Critical cofactor for transamination reactions in non-essential amino acid synthesis. Folic Acid B9 (152mcg per serving, 27% RDA): Required for purine and pyrimidine synthesis, facilitating rapid cellular proliferation during epithelialisation. Vitamin B12 Cyanocobalamin (0.32mcg per serving, 27% RDA): Supports erythropoiesis and neural recovery following surgical trauma. Vitamin D Cholecalciferol (60 IU per serving, 10% RDA): Modulates immune cell function and osteoblast activity in musculoskeletal and soft tissue healing — critical for sternal bone healing post-sternotomy (Vitamin D deficiency affects up to 70% of cardiac surgical patients). Calcium (0.323mg per serving, 32.33% RDA): Secondary messenger in inflammatory phase of wound healing and skeletal mineralisation. Phosphorus (0.253mg per serving, 25.33% RDA): Required for ATP synthesis and structural cellular membranes.

knowledge_sections.title: Department-Specific Clinical Applications
knowledge_sections.content: ORTHOPEDIC SURGERY (ACL reconstruction, THA, TKA): Post-operative immobilisation induces rapid quadriceps atrophy through reduced basal and postprandial muscle protein synthesis rates. L-Leucine + SPI provides mTORC1 trigger and complete EAA substrate to mitigate disuse atrophy and support rehabilitation benchmarks. L-Arginine + Vitamin C drives collagen matrix deposition for ligament, tendon, and joint capsule repair. Vitamin D supports osteoblast-mediated bone mineralisation. UROLOGY / PROSTATECTOMY: Post-prostatectomy cavernous nerve neuropraxia impairs endogenous NO release, causing cavernous tissue hypoxia and fibrotic changes. L-Arginine as eNOS substrate maintains cavernous microvascular perfusion, limits collagenous degeneration, and preserves smooth muscle elasticity during the nerve regeneration window. Mucosal anastomosis of the bladder neck relies on optimal local microperfusion. GENERAL SURGERY (laparotomy, bowel resection, hernia repair): Protein requirement of 1.2-2.0g/kg ideal body weight per day. SPI + L-Leucine limits skeletal muscle proteolysis. L-Arginine enhances T-lymphocyte proliferation to reduce infectious complications and shorten hospital stay. Vitamin C and Copper catalyse fascial collagen synthesis and cross-linking to prevent incisional hernia formation. GYNECOLOGY / HYSTERECTOMY: Pelvic floor musculature (levator ani, coccygeus) must heal under continuous intra-abdominal pressure. Post-operative constipation poses mechanical risk to sutured pelvic floor and vaginal cuff. SPI's high digestibility avoids delayed gastric emptying and reduces constipation — protecting the healing pelvic floor. L-Arginine + Vitamin C accelerate collagen remodelling in endopelvic fascia and uterosacral ligaments. SURGICAL ONCOLOGY: Cancer cachexia patients present with tumour-induced IL-1beta and TNF-alpha driving hypermetabolism and muscle proteolysis. L-Leucine counteracts tumour-induced mTORC1 suppression. SPI provides non-tumour-promoting plant-based protein for hepatic acute-phase synthesis. L-Arginine immunomodulates T-cell receptor expression, counteracting immunosuppression from oncological therapies. GASTROINTESTINAL SURGERY (colectomy, pancreaticoduodenectomy): Post-operative hypoalbuminaemia affects 20-40% of patients via protein-losing enteropathy and intestinal tight junction failure. SPI downregulates mucosal inflammatory cytokines (IL-4, IL-13) and stabilises polymeric immunoglobulin receptors and intestinal mucins to prevent hyperpermeability, albumin extravasation, interstitial oedema, and third-spacing. NEUROSURGERY / TBI: Craniotomy and traumatic brain injury can elevate resting energy expenditure 87-200% above baseline. L-Arginine maintains cerebral microvascular perfusion and blood-brain barrier integrity. SPI provides complete amino acids without rapid glucose spikes that can exacerbate secondary neuronal ischaemia. B-complex vitamins support axonal repair and cognitive recovery. VASCULAR SURGERY: L-Arginine prevents cNOS uncoupling during ischemia-reperfusion injury — replenishing intracellular substrate pool to shift from toxic superoxide radical production back to protective NO synthesis, reducing reperfusion vasoconstriction and microvascular oedema, improving graft and arteriovenous fistula patency and limb salvage rates. CARDIOTHORACIC SURGERY (sternotomy): Vitamin D supplementation critical for sternal osteogenesis. Vitamin D deficiency affects up to 70% of cardiac patients and is associated with impaired osteoblast function. SPI-derived amino acids with Vitamin C and Copper accelerate collagenous osteoid matrix synthesis for sternal stability. Very low fat (0.19g per serving) and zero sucrose are compatible with strict cardiac fluid restriction and heart-healthy diet protocols. PLASTIC AND RECONSTRUCTIVE SURGERY (free flap, skin graft): L-Arginine via NO-mediated vasodilation optimises microvascular capillary perfusion to prevent marginal flap necrosis, venous congestion, and flap loss. Arginase pathway provides polyamines and proline for collagen remodelling. Vitamin C and Iron as cofactors for prolyl-4-hydroxylase ensure correct dermal extracellular matrix cross-linking. ENT SURGERY / TONSILLECTOMY: Odynophagia (painful swallowing) restricts solid food for up to two weeks. Surgicover is smooth, low-viscosity, non-acidic, and non-irritating to raw pharyngeal mucosa — can be administered as liquid without mastication via cup, syringe, or feeding tube. Vitamin C and micronutrients accelerate mucosal re-epithelialisation to reduce delayed haemorrhage risk (peaks post-operative days 4-10). DENTAL AND MAXILLOFACIAL SURGERY / JAW WIRING: Patients on liquid-only diet for 4-8 weeks. Surgicover easily swallowed through gaps in orthodontic elastics or wired teeth. SPI + L-Leucine prevents masseter and temporalis muscle wasting during jaw fixation. Calcium, Phosphorus, and Vitamin D drive bone callus formation at osteotomy or fracture sites.

knowledge_sections.title: Dosing Protocols and Administration
knowledge_sections.content: Standard serving: 1 heaped scoop = 20g powder in 150-200ml lukewarm water or milk. Prehabilitation phase (5-7 days pre-surgery): 1 serving twice daily (BID) = 400mg L-Arginine, 200mg L-Leucine, 12g protein per day. Post-operative Day 2-5: 1 serving twice daily (BID). Post-operative Day 6 onwards: 1-2 servings daily. Hypercatabolic states (major GI, orthopaedic, oncological surgery): titrate to three times daily (TID) or four times daily (QID) = 18-24g protein, 600-800mg L-Arginine, 300-400mg L-Leucine per day. Post-exercise dosing: consume 20-30 minutes after physical therapy to optimise mTORC1-mediated muscle protein synthesis. For ENT, dental, and maxillofacial patients: administer via cup, syringe, or feeding tube. Extended home recovery: continue 1-2 servings per day for 14-30 days post-discharge. Avoid large protein boluses over 30g in a single dose — causes delayed gastric emptying, nausea, abdominal distension, and the muscle-full amino acid saturation effect.

knowledge_sections.title: Safety, Contraindications, and Special Populations
knowledge_sections.content: SAFE FOR: Type 1 and Type 2 diabetic patients (zero added sucrose, sucralose GI=0). Post-operative patients with secondary lactose intolerance (lactose less than 0.1g per serving, far below the 12g clinical threshold). ENT and oral surgery patients (smooth, low-viscosity, non-acidic). Cardiac and vascular patients (0.19g fat per serving, zero sucrose). CKD CAUTION: In patients with chronic kidney disease Stage 3 or higher, total daily protein intake including Surgicover must be factored into the overall renal diet plan. Phosphorus (0.253mg per serving) and Calcium (0.323mg per serving) must be monitored via regular serum electrolytes and renal function tests. Use under direction of a clinical nephrologist or dietitian only. NOT FOR: Parenteral or intravenous use. Consumption within 2 hours before anaesthesia (protein and fat content prevents safe gastric emptying within NPO window). TEMPERATURE: Do not mix with liquids above 40C / 104F. ALLERGENS: Contains Milk and Soy — must be prominently declared on packaging per FSSAI Packaging and Labelling Regulations.

knowledge_sections.title: How Surgicover Compares to Other Clinical Nutrition Products
knowledge_sections.content: Versus Ensure High Protein: Ensure uses Milk Protein Concentrate and Soy Protein Isolate. Contains lower sucrose, approximately 10mg Vitamin C, approximately 30 IU Vitamin D. No added free-form L-Arginine or L-Leucine. Surgicover advantage: targeted free-form dual amino acid spike (L-Arginine 200mg + L-Leucine 100mg per serving) for direct NOS and mTORC1 activation, zero sucrose versus lower sucrose, higher Vitamin C and Iron. Versus PentaSure HP: Uses Whey Protein Concentrate; contains fructose and maltodextrin (both glycaemic concerns in surgical patients). No added L-Arginine or L-Leucine beyond inherent protein content. Surgicover advantage: lactose-free primary protein with superior GI tolerance, zero glycaemic load sweetener, targeted amino acid spike for wound and muscle clinical targets. Versus Resource High Protein by Nestle: Whey Protein Concentrate and Skimmed Milk Powder; contains maltodextrin (high GI), no added L-Arginine or L-Leucine. Surgicover advantage: SPI-based lactose-compatible protein, surgical-specific amino acid fortification, zero added sucrose with sucralose. Surgicover unique positioning: Only supplement in this category specifically engineered for the peri-operative window with a dual free-form L-Arginine and L-Leucine spike, zero sucrose sweetened with sucralose, and a lactose-compatible hybrid SPI protein matrix.

knowledge_sections.title: Frequently Asked Questions
knowledge_sections.content: Q: Is Surgicover safe for diabetic patients? A: Yes. Zero added sucrose, sweetened exclusively with sucralose (GI=0). Safe for Type 1 and Type 2 diabetics. The Diabetic Cover variant is specifically designed for diabetic surgical patients. Q: Does the Skimmed Milk Powder compromise the lactose-free claim? A: No. SMP is a minor component providing methionine and improving dispersibility. Lactose per 20g serving is less than 0.1g — far below the 12g clinical threshold for secondary lactose intolerance. Q: Is 6g protein per serving enough? A: The 20g serving is designed for clinical titration. At TID or QID dosing (3-4 servings per day), it delivers 18-24g of bioavailable protein daily in small manageable volumes that prevent gastric distension in post-operative gastroparesis. Fractionated dosing provides sustained mTOR activation versus a single large bolus. Q: Why is sucralose used instead of honey or fructose? A: Honey and fructose are high-glycaemic carbohydrates that cause rapid blood glucose spikes in patients already experiencing surgical insulin resistance. Sucralose is non-caloric and non-glycaemic. Q: Can CKD patients use Surgicover? A: In CKD Stage 3 or higher, use must be carefully monitored by a nephrologist or clinical dietitian — protein, phosphorus, and calcium intake must all be factored into the renal diet plan. Q: Can Surgicover be mixed with hot water or tea? A: No. Must be mixed with lukewarm or room-temperature liquids only (below 40C / 104F) to prevent protein denaturation and vitamin degradation. Q: When should I start taking Surgicover? A: 5-7 days before surgery for prehabilitation, and resume from post-operative day 2 (after clinical clearance on clear liquid diet on day 1). Continue for 14-30 days post-discharge. Q: Can Surgicover be used as a tube feed? A: For patients with restricted chewing (ENT, dental, maxillofacial), Surgicover can be administered via cup, syringe, or feeding tube. It is not formulated as a complete tube-feed formula — standard clinical nutrition protocols for enteral tube feeding should be followed. Q: What does PDCAAS 1.0 mean? A: Protein Digestibility-Corrected Amino Acid Score of 1.0 is the highest possible score, indicating the protein satisfies 100% of the human body's indispensable amino acid requirements. Surgicover's hybrid soya-milk protein blend achieves PDCAAS 1.0. Q: Does Surgicover cause constipation? A: No. Unlike dense animal proteins, SPI does not delay gastric emptying or prolong transit time. This is especially important in gynecological and abdominal surgical patients where postoperative constipation poses a mechanical risk to sutured wound sites.
